stories.title: Creator produces initial requirements draft
stories.goal: have the requirements-analyst produce a labeled Round 1 draft when debate mode is active
stories.benefit: the Critic has a concrete artifact to review rather than reviewing nothing

acceptance_criteria.given: debate mode is ON and the user has provided a feature description
acceptance_criteria.then: the Creator (requirements-analyst) produces requirements-spec.md, user-stories.json, nfr-matrix.md, and traceability-matrix.csv labeled as Round 1 Draft
the requirements-analyst produces artifacts using the current single-agent flow unchanged

acceptance_criteria.given: the user provided a rich feature description from BACKLOG.md
acceptance_criteria.then: the Creator reflects back the description and asks ONE targeted follow-up question instead of 3 generic questions

stories.title: Critic reviews and challenges requirements
stories.goal: have a dedicated Critic agent review Creator's output and identify defects
stories.benefit: vague ACs, unmeasured NFRs, and missing edge cases are caught before they propagate to later phases

acceptance_criteria.given: the Creator has produced Round N artifacts
acceptance_criteria.then: the Critic produces a structured critique with findings classified as BLOCKING or WARNING, each referencing a specific requirement/AC/NFR ID

acceptance_criteria.given: an AC uses vague language (e.g., 'should handle errors appropriately')
acceptance_criteria.then: the Critic flags it as BLOCKING with a specific recommendation for Given/When/Then rewrite

acceptance_criteria.given: an NFR has no quantified metric (e.g., 'system should be fast')
acceptance_criteria.then: the Critic flags it as BLOCKING with a recommendation for specific measurable target

acceptance_criteria.given: a requirement has no linked user story
acceptance_criteria.when: the Critic reviews the traceability matrix
acceptance_criteria.then: the Critic flags it as BLOCKING (orphan requirement)

acceptance_criteria.when: the Critic reviews the requirements set
acceptance_criteria.then: the Critic flags both as BLOCKING with an explanation of the contradiction

stories.title: Refiner addresses Critic findings
stories.goal: have a Refiner agent take the Critic's findings and produce improved artifacts
stories.benefit: all blocking defects are resolved before artifacts are finalized

acceptance_criteria.given: the Critic produced N BLOCKING findings
acceptance_criteria.then: the Refiner produces updated artifacts with all N BLOCKING findings addressed and documented in a change log
the AC is rewritten in Given/When/Then format with specific, testable conditions
the NFR is rewritten with a quantified metric (e.g., 'p95 < 200ms', '>= 80% coverage')

acceptance_criteria.given: the Refiner cannot resolve a finding without user input
acceptance_criteria.then: the finding is escalated with [NEEDS CLARIFICATION] marker per Article IV

stories.title: Debate loop converges within max rounds
stories.goal: have the debate loop terminate when the Critic finds zero blocking issues or after max 3 rounds
stories.benefit: I get high-quality requirements without infinite processing

acceptance_criteria.when: the Critic produces zero BLOCKING findings (warnings allowed)
acceptance_criteria.then: the debate converges and final artifacts are saved as the standard artifact names

acceptance_criteria.when: the Critic still has BLOCKING findings
acceptance_criteria.then: the loop terminates, latest artifacts are saved, and a warning is appended noting unconverged findings

acceptance_criteria.given: the debate converges at round N
acceptance_criteria.then: a debate-summary.md is created showing round count, findings per round, and changes made
a round-N-critique.md file is saved in the artifact folder for audit trail visibility

stories.title: Control debate mode via flags
stories.goal: enable or disable debate mode using CLI flags and workflow settings
stories.benefit: I can choose the right level of rigor for each feature

acceptance_criteria.given: a standard feature workflow without flags
acceptance_criteria.then: debate mode is ON by default
debate mode is OFF (single-agent, current behavior)
debate mode is OFF regardless of sizing
debate mode is ON (explicit override takes precedence)
active_workflow.debate_mode is set in state.json (true or false)

stories.title: Orchestrator manages debate delegation
stories.goal: have the orchestrator route between Creator, Critic, and Refiner correctly based on debate mode
stories.benefit: debate mode integrates seamlessly into the existing phase lifecycle

acceptance_criteria.then: the orchestrator runs Creator -> Critic -> Refiner loop (up to 3 rounds) before marking Phase 01 complete
the orchestrator delegates to requirements-analyst as a single agent (current behavior)
active_workflow.debate_state is updated with round_number, convergence_status, and blocking_findings_count

stories.title: Conversational context-aware Creator
stories.goal: have the Creator use the feature description as context instead of starting with cold generic questions
stories.benefit: requirements capture is faster and more engaging, reducing wasted turns on already-known information

acceptance_criteria.given: the user provided a feature description in the /isdlc feature command
acceptance_criteria.then: the Creator's first response acknowledges and summarizes the provided description

acceptance_criteria.given: the feature description already covers problem, users, and success criteria
acceptance_criteria.then: the Creator skips the 3 generic opening questions and asks a targeted follow-up about what's missing

acceptance_criteria.given: discovery_context exists in state.json and is fresh (< 24h)
acceptance_criteria.then: the Creator uses discovery data (tech stack, test coverage, architecture) as known baseline and only asks about what's new
